summary.finding: Broad AI/chat provider endpoints. SSRF protections present in gateway paths.
Optional OTel plugin can export traces/metrics externally when enabled.
Android app — camera, mic, location, SMS, install packages. Chrome extension: debugger + tabs.
eval() / new Function() on user-reachable input. Shell execution framework by design.
0 CVEs (pnpm audit). 58/73 deps use floating ranges. Beta/pre-release packages present.
curl | bash pattern in macOS installer and docker smoke tests.
gradle-wrapper.jar committed; no in-repo provenance attestation.
228k★, SECURITY.md present, but main branch unprotected, mixed signing.

items.title: eval() / new Function() on attacker-reachable input
items.description: Browser interaction tooling constructs and evaluates function bodies dynamically. The intended use is browser-context automation, but if attacker-controlled input reaches these code paths, arbitrary code execution is the result — with no sandbox.

items.title: Shell execution framework — central to node-host
items.description: Process execution is a core feature. Policy/allowlist machinery exists but is non-trivial; misconfiguration allows arbitrary command execution with the user's full privileges.

items.title: Remote installer: curl … | bash
items.description: The macOS app installer builds and executes a remote shell script via curl | bash. This gives the install process full host execution privileges from an unverified remote source — MitM or CDN compromise leads directly to RCE. Same pattern appears in docker smoke/e2e test scripts.

checks.text: prepare script in package.json:106 modifies git hooks path at install time.
Lockfile (pnpm-lock.yaml) exists; overrides include security-related package pins.

items.title: Chrome extension: debugger + tabs — near-total browser control
items.description: The extension requests debugger, tabs, activeTab, storage, alarms, and webNavigation. Host permissions are loopback-only, but the debugger + tabs combination enables read/write access to all browser tabs, including credential fields and authenticated sessions.

items.title: Android: camera, mic, location, SMS, package install
items.description: Android manifest requests five categories of sensitive permissions. Cleartext traffic is also permitted in defined contexts. The combination makes this app unsuitable for deployment on managed corporate devices.

redFlags.notes: No convincing malicious obfuscation in runtime code

redFlags.check: Unexplained network calls to unknown domains
redFlags.notes: Broad endpoint set; most map to integrations. Some placeholder hosts appear.
Extension + Android permissions are broad and sensitive
OTel plugin can export data when enabled; config-gated
None found in scanned runtime paths

remediation: Run only in an isolated VM or container with no privileged corp credentials or host mounts.
Enforce outbound network allowlists — permit only provider domains strictly required for your use case.
Disable or remove the OTel diagnostics plugin unless explicitly needed.
Disable remote installer one-liners. Use pinned artifact + checksum/SBOM verification instead of curl | bash.
Restrict exec tooling by policy — strict allowlists and explicit approvals before any shell execution.
Verify gradle-wrapper.jar provenance in CI via Gradle wrapper validation and checksum policy.
Prefer tagged releases and pinned commits. Require branch protection and signed-release verification for internal adoption.
